events.description: Turtle Mountain Public Domain Allotments made under Act of April 21, 1904 (33 Stat. 189-194)

Trust patents issued for allotments MC-026534 (Eliza Peltier, No. 523765), MC-026515 (Alexander Decoteau Jr., No. 523764), MC-026540 (Pierre Nadeau, No. 523766), MC-026507 (Louis Decoteau, No. 523763)
Probate of Eliza Peltier, Probate No. 50999-17
Probate of Louis Decoteau, Probate No. 57962-17
Probate of Jeanette Decoteau, Probate No. 44516-38
Probate of John B. Decoteau, Probate No. 15803-39
Probate of Mary Louise Raney Gendron, Probate Nos. C-262-52 and 287-50
Probate of Alexander Emil Gourneau, Probate Nos. C-69-56 and 3175-56
John Mahnken leased allotment 772 (Nadeau/Brownfield area) for period 5-1-40 to 4-30-43 at ten cents per acre
Major leased Louis Decoteau allotment MC-026507 for period 1940-1943
John Mahnken lease on allotment 772 expired
Charles Johnston leased Alexander Gourneau allotment MC-026695 for period 1940-1943
Major leased Louis Decoteau allotment again until 1945; no lease or payment since then
Major's lease on Louis Decoteau allotment expired; no renewal or payment since
Ben Brownfield of Ridgeway acquired lease/holdings on allotment 772 area after Mahnken
Major held approved lease on Eliza Peltier allotment expiring November 30, 1952; Florence Peltier (heir) obtained this recent lease
Fee patents already processed for Florence Peltier Voo (MC-026746) and Stilma (Gourneau) Albert (MC-026696)
Report of inspection of Turtle Mountain Public Domain Allotments in Carter County, Montana, by J.C. Schubert, Extension Agent, Northern Cheyenne Agency
Application for sale filed by allottee in August 1953 (allotment 772 area)
Chet Hamilton wrote to Indian Lands office inquiring about leasing lands in T.1N, R.58E, Carter County
Billings Area Office forwarded Hamilton's letter to Carl L. Pearson, Northern Cheyenne Agency
Response to Hamilton inquiry indicating most land privately owned except SE/4NE/4 Sec. 14, T.1N, R.58E which is USA Forest land
Carl L. Pearson wrote to Superintendent of Turtle Mountain Agency regarding public domain allotments, suggesting supervised sales and fee patents
Turtle Mountain Consolidated Agency responded to carbon copy of Pearson's May 5 letter regarding public domain allotments, correcting township description
Knute H. Lee, Superintendent Turtle Mountain Agency, responded to Pearson's May 17 letter offering assistance with fee patent applications and supervised sales
Carl L. Pearson wrote to Pierre Nadeau suggesting sale of his allotment due to trespass and fencing difficulties in Carter County
Harold E. Olson, Acting Superintendent, wrote to Carl L. Pearson enclosing signed petition for sale of Pierre Nadeau's 40 acres
Pierre Nadeau signed petition for sale of SE/4NE/4 Sec. 22, T.4S, R.58E, 40 acres, before Acting Superintendent H.E. Olson
Carl L. Pearson wrote to Area Director, Billings, regarding Turtle Mountain allotments, requesting appraisal assistance
Carl L. Pearson wrote to Harold W. Schunk requesting ownership statements for five Turtle Mountain allotments
Harold W. Schunk responded to Pearson's September 25 letter, enclosing ownership statements for five allotments and noting only Pierre Nadeau had applied for sale
Ownership statements prepared and certified for five allotments: MC-026534, MC-026515, MC-026540, MC-026507, MC-026695

financial_transactions.description: Lease of allotment 772 area for period 5-1-40 to 4-30-43 at ten cents per acre
Pierre Nadeau previously sold part of allotment or inherited land; bought a home and north 10 acres with the money
Pierre Nadeau's small share in father's estate, about 80 acres, value approximately $300.00
Appraised value of allotment 428C (Alexander Gourneau MC-026695 partial), 160 acres at $2.50/acre
Appraised value of allotment 428C NW/4 Sec. 22, T.3S, R.58E, 160 acres at $3.25/acre
Appraised value of Alexander Gourneau allotment at $3.25/acre or $320.00 (second valuation reference)
Appraised value of allotment 278-3 (Alexander Decoteau MC-026515), 160 acres at $2.50/acre
Appraised value of 40-acre portion SE/4NE/4 Sec. 22 (Pierre Nadeau allotment) at $2.50/acre
Appraised value of allotment 772 (Pierre Nadeau), Lots 4,5,6,7 Sec. 31 and Lots 3,4 Sec. 32, 159.94 acres at $2.00/acre
Appraised value of Louis Decoteau allotment NE/4 Sec. 8, 160 acres at $2.50/acre

fee_patents.acreage: 160 total; 40 acres subject to sale petition

correspondence.subject: Leasing Indian trust lands in T.1N, R.58E, Carter County, Montana
correspondence.action_requested: Information on leasing Indian trust allotment lands
correspondence.outcome: Forwarded to Carl L. Pearson by Billings Area Office; response indicated most land privately owned except SE/4NE/4 Sec. 14 which is USA Forest land

correspondence.sender_title: Acting Area Director, Billings Area Office
correspondence.subject: Hamilton letter regarding leasing lands on Northern Cheyenne Reservation
correspondence.action_requested: Advise Mr. Hamilton on leasing matter and provide copy to Billings office

correspondence.subject: Public domain Turtle Mountain Chippewa allotments in Carter County, Montana
correspondence.action_requested: Assist allottees in making fee patent applications; prepare petitions for supervised sales for deceased allottees; allow reservation of oil/mineral rights
correspondence.outcome: Knute H. Lee responded June 3, 1954 offering assistance

correspondence.subject: Public domain Turtle Mountain Chippewa allotments
correspondence.action_requested: Offered assistance with fee patent applications and supervised sale petitions including oil/gas/mineral rights reservations

correspondence.subject: Public domain allotments in Carter County; correction of township description
correspondence.action_requested: Corrected township description from T.3S/4S to proper townships; offered information on leasing through Pearson's records
correspondence.outcome: Hamilton informed of correct land descriptions and leasing process

correspondence.subject: Appendix on Turtle Mountain allotments; request for appraisal assistance
correspondence.action_requested: Requested special attention to appraisal of Turtle Mountain public domain allotments if appraiser visits Billings area

correspondence.subject: Difficulty with Carter County range allotment due to fencing law and trespass
correspondence.action_requested: Suggested Nadeau consider selling his allotment; offered to help prepare sale application papers
correspondence.outcome: Nadeau visited Turtle Mountain Agency and signed petition for sale on May 4, 1955

correspondence.subject: Petition for sale of Pierre Nadeau's 40-acre allotment SE/4NE/4 Sec. 22, T.4S, R.58E
correspondence.action_requested: Enclosed signed petition for advertising 40-acre parcel for sale

correspondence.subject: Five Turtle Mountain Public Domain allotments under Northern Cheyenne Agency jurisdiction
correspondence.action_requested: Requested ownership statements for allotments MC-026695, MC-026507, MC-026534, MC-026515, MC-026540; referenced letters of May 17, 1954 and report of September 14, 1953
correspondence.outcome: Schunk responded October 8, 1956 with ownership statements enclosed

correspondence.subject: Turtle Mountain Public Domain Allotments ownership statements
correspondence.action_requested: Enclosed ownership statements for five allotments; noted only Pierre Nadeau had applied for sale; offered to prepare applications for other allottees or heirs wishing to sell
correspondence.outcome: Ownership statements provided; further action pending from allottees/heirs

legislative_actions.outcome: Act authorizing Turtle Mountain Public Domain Allotments; allotments made April 21, 1904
